_meta.what_firm_does: Unit is a market leader in embedded finance—offering ready-to-launch financial services (banking, capital) to ~100 customers (Wix, Honeybook, Relay) serving millions of end-users. Moves $40B+/yr, 12M+ API calls/day. Raised $160M+ (Insight, Accel). NYC office.
_meta.jd_wants: Own tools/workflows/processes powering support; design automations + leverage AI to handle repetitive work at scale; own support tech stack (ticketing, KB, integrations); analyze support data → root causes → product/process improvements; partner w/ Eng/Product on systemic issues; triage complex inquiries; own customer↔Product feedback loop. 1-2 yrs support ops or building AI/automated workflows; Zapier/custom scripts/AI building; quick learner; process-improvement mindset; fintech enthusiasm.
_meta.drafted_by: Opus 4.8 in Jamie's voice, mined from voice corpus + feedback rules
_meta.honest_gap_note: Stretch — JD wants hands-on automation building (Zapier/scripts). Jamie has strong process-improvement + data + AI-tool-use + a real RAG side-build, but not production automation engineering. Candid in answers.
_meta.status: DRAFT — Jamie to review/edit before submit

Why are you interested in this role?: This role is basically my instinct made into a job. I'm the person who can't help asking 'why does this exist?' and 'how could this be automated?'—and I love turning a messy, manual process into something clean and scalable. At InGenius I design and continuously improve workflows for 20+ programs, identify repetitive work, and deploy AI tools to handle it at scale, which cut manual effort and onboarding time by 75%. Owning the tools and workflows behind a support function, and using AI to make them smarter, is exactly the kind of high-leverage operations work I want to do.
Tell us about your experience with autom: I'll be candid up front: I haven't built production automations in Zapier or with custom scripts, so that specific tooling would be a ramp for me. But I'm genuinely hands-on with AI—I use AI tools daily and have been building a RAG-based system on the side that evaluates job fit from my own resumes and experience, so I think about automation concretely, not abstractly. At InGenius I already identify repetitive workflows and deploy AI tools to increase capacity and quality. I'm a fast learner, and the core instinct—spot the friction, build the fix, measure whether it worked—is already how I operate.
Tell us about a time you diagnosed a roo: At Vestas I noticed onboarding for new hires was inconsistent across regions and generating repeat questions. Rather than just answer them, I interviewed cross-regional stakeholders, compared the actual workflow against the global standard, pinpointed the misalignment, and built a checklist-based process with a clear before/after walkthrough of the why. It reduced both onboarding time and complaints. That pattern—diagnose with data before designing, fix the underlying system rather than the symptom—is exactly what I'd bring to surfacing trends in support data and turning them into product and process improvements.
Why Unit / fintech?: I'm drawn to Unit because embedded finance is genuinely foundational infrastructure—you're moving $40B+ a year and powering financial services for millions of people through your customers—and the mission of a more equitable financial ecosystem resonates with me. I also love that this role sits close to Engineering and Product, where I'd own the feedback loop that makes the voice of the customer actually shape the roadmap. My training in Applied Organizational Psychology means I treat operations as a system to study and improve, which is exactly what a builder's support-ops role calls for.
